---
title: "[SOLVED] Problem with xorg.conf causing system to run in low graphics mode."
date: 2008-06-12
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12
I am having a problem with my system starting in low graphics mode.  It does not load the nvidia driver even though it shows it in xorg.conf.  I am listing my xorg.conf file below.  If you can find anything wrong with it please let me know so I can make the appropriate changes to it.
```
terminator@terminator-desktop:~$ cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@vernadsky)  Tue Mar  4 20:24:34 UTC 2008

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Tue Jan 22 19:53:46 PST 2008
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Delta"
    ModelName      "De-570"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 120.0
    ModeLine       "1280x768_70.00" 95.0 1280 1352 1488 1696 768 769 772 800 -hsync +vsync
    Modeline       "1280x960_65.00"  110.95  1280 1360 1496 1712  960 961 964 997  -HSync +Vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce FX 5200"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x768_70 +0+0" "1280x960_65 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

```

I would really like to get it to run in 1280x960 mode.  If I leave off the 1280x960_65 in the modeline and metamodes then the system works perfectly.

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12
I found if I get rid of the 1280x768_70 in the modeline and metamode section then the system will boot at 1280x960_65.  Now I have a problem where my screen will not completely fill.  I have about a 3/4" area on the right and left parts of the screen blank.  I have adjusted the horizontal control to 100% and that did not help.  Any suggestions would be appreciated.

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12

### Post by KaliVoid on 2008-06-12
hi

did you play with the kernel restricted modules ?
when i did (:)), i had the same problems as you describe.

if so, open synaptic and search for "linux restricted modules" and check if "linux-restricted-modules-(your kernel version)"is installed.

to find your kernel version type in terminal
```
uname -r
```

---

I am running the 2.6.24-18-generic kernel.  All options that were checked with the version 16 drivers are now checked with the version 18 drivers.  Still have screen with problem.

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12
The nvidia driver is now booting properly.  The screen size of 1280x960_70 is working.
Only problem is that the screen displayed does not fill the monitors entire viewing area.
> # nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@vernadsky)  Tue Mar  4 20:24:34 UTC 2008

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

# values from the debconf database.

### Post by bijeeshvs on 2008-06-12
the problem is your screen refresh rate try lowering it then the screen will fill to its sides.............
hope u r using a CRT monitor.................

may be a refresh rate around 60 should work fine..............

---

### Post by ukripper on 2008-06-12
you sure your hsync vertsync are exactly matching the monitor recommended? 
>  HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 120.0
How about trying this/
```
HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0
VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
```

tinker abit with above and find correct setting which fits your screen

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12
If I lower it to 60 or 55 won't I get screen flicker.  And yes I am running a 14" CRT monitor. I checked the Delta De-570 specs and the refresh rate and horizontal sync are:
> Horiz 30-70
Vert 50-120

---

### Post by bijeeshvs on 2008-06-12
if its a 14" monitor and of normal type (not wide screen) u should try 1024x768 resolution i think, thats the best resolution for u please give it a try


@ 60 htz u get more flicker than @ 70 htz but its not that annoying..............................

try using the nvidia settings for changing the resolution...............

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-06-12
Did what you suggested bijeeshvs and the monitor filled out.  Thought I could run that higher resolution, but I guess not.  Thanks again......
